summary: The TypeScript package structure is well-implemented with proper package.json configuration, TypeScript compiler settings, and build scripts. The code follows TypeScript best practices and provides a solid foundation for the NPM package.

observations: Package.json is comprehensive with all required fields (name, version, description, license, author, repository, bugs, homepage, keywords, engines)
Proper ESM/CommonJS dual-mode exports with correct path mappings
CLI binary entry point properly configured
TypeScript config uses strict mode with comprehensive compiler options
Dual tsconfig setup for ESM (tsconfig.esm.json) and CommonJS (tsconfig.cjs.json) outputs
Build scripts are well-organized with clean, esm, and cjs phases
Dev dependencies include TypeScript 5.3+ and oxlint for linting
Path aliases configured for clean imports (@/*, @quantization/*, etc.)
TypeScript configuration targets ES2022 with NodeNext module resolution
Strict type checking enabled (strict: true, noImplicitAny: true, etc.)
Source maps and declaration maps generated for debugging
RootDir and outDir properly configured to separate source from build
Main entry exports correctly point to dist/cjs and dist/esm
Type definitions properly exported via types field
Tests exist but are focused on quantization functionality rather than package structure
No specific tests for CLI argument parsing or package exports
Test infrastructure uses bun:test which is appropriate for Bun runtime
58 tests passing across 4 test files with good coverage of quantization features

recommendations.description: Consider adding integration tests for CLI commands (download, quantize, serve, list, remove)
Add a simple smoke test that verifies package exports work correctly after build
